description: Represents a single finding from a constitutional audit check (src/shared/models/audit_models.py). AuditFinding is the persistence surface for audit_runs.findings JSONB — one of the three Finding surfaces ADR-056 identified. The details property is a backward-compat alias for context; it is not a declared field and is not governed here. A separate governance entry will govern AuditFinding against the canonical Finding nucleus (Finding.json), which will produce drift findings for missing nucleus fields (rule_id, subject, worker_uuid absent; check_id/message serve analogous roles under different names). Severity vocabulary is governed by audit_severity in .intent/META/enums.json (ADR-059 D2): info/low/medium/high/block. AuditSeverity IntEnum and CIM CheckResult.severity now share this vocabulary. Authority: ADR-056 Wave 1.

check_id.description: The constitutional rule check identifier that produced this finding. Analogous to rule_id in the Finding nucleus; name difference is documented drift.

severity.description: Severity level. AuditSeverity IntEnum (INFO=1, LOW=2, MEDIUM=3, HIGH=4, BLOCK=5) stored as lowercase string via __str__. Vocabulary governed by audit_severity enum per ADR-059 D2.

message.description: Human-readable description of the finding. Analogous to evidence in the Finding nucleus.

file_path.description: Repo-relative file path where the finding was detected (str | None). Absent for findings not tied to a specific file.

line_number.description: Line number within file_path where the finding was detected (int | None).

context.description: Structured finding data. Single source of truth; the details property is a backward-compat alias. Populated from both context= and details= constructor kwargs.
